purpose: Choose Transaction Script, Domain Model, Table Module, Service Layer, or a mixed/refactoring path.
ask_if_missing: Are the rules mostly CRUD/workflow, or do they have many interacting special cases?
Do platform tools revolve around tabular record sets?
Will more than one client/API invoke the same business operations?
assume_if_low_risk: refactor Transaction Script toward Domain Model when duplication grows

branches.criteria: Simple business logic, few special cases, fast delivery priority
branches.recommendation: Use Transaction Script with separated data-source access.

branches.criteria: Complex or volatile business rules with duplication pressure
branches.recommendation: Use Domain Model and plan for Data Mapper or equivalent O/R mapping.

branches.criteria: Record Set-centric platform/UI and table-shaped behavior
branches.recommendation: Use Table Module with Table Data Gateway.

branches.criteria: Multiple clients or transaction/security/workflow coordination across resources
branches.recommendation: Add Service Layer; keep it thin over Domain Model where possible or use operation scripts for application coordination.

failure_modes: Transaction Scripts accumulate duplicated conditional logic
Domain Model adds mapping complexity without enough domain complexity to repay it
Table Module is chosen without Record Set/tool support
Service Layer absorbs domain logic that belongs in reusable domain objects
